tasks.title: Maintain the Sacramento capture target ledger
tasks.purpose: Rank which Sacramento sites and site clusters should be captured first based on current robot workflow focus, buyer value, and access realism.
done_when: The Sacramento target ledger names the first proof candidates, queued lawful-access buckets, and longer-horizon discovery lanes.
Capture priorities stay tied to current robot workflow demand and lawful access realism instead of generic city coverage.

tasks.title: Maintain the Sacramento parallel lawful-access queue
tasks.purpose: Keep a multi-site lawful-access queue active so Sacramento warehouse and facility work does not stall on a single signature path.
done_when: Sacramento keeps 3-5 named lawful-access candidates or buyer-linked fallback sites queued in parallel, with one current next step per candidate.
If one warehouse stalls, another named access path is ready without restarting city planning from zero.
Each queued candidate names the current access posture, likely owner/operator/tenant path, and whether the next move belongs to buyer thread, operator intro, or existing lawful access.

tasks.title: Lock Sacramento source policy and invite/access-code posture
tasks.purpose: Keep Sacramento sourcing narrow, truthful, and off the founder lane for routine approvals while explicitly distinguishing private controlled interiors from public, non-controlled commercial capture.
done_when: Sacramento source policy names allowed channels, disallowed channels, referral rules, and who may issue invites or access codes.
Sacramento source policy makes public, non-controlled commercial community sourcing explicit while keeping private controlled interiors on stricter lawful-access paths.
Sacramento source policy names the online habitats for the public commercial lane instead of leaving community sourcing abstract.
Routine invite/access-code decisions stay with Growth Lead and Ops Lead inside written guardrails.

tasks.title: Run Sacramento site-operator partnership routing
tasks.purpose: Prepare the operator-side access path for Sacramento warehouses and facilities by identifying contacts, operator value props, approval sequence, and escalation boundaries before the city waits on a single site.
done_when: Sacramento operator-lane packet identifies likely owner/operator/tenant contacts, operator-side value props, and the exact approval sequence for the highest-priority warehouse/facility candidates.
The first operator-outreach draft or intro packet is ready for human review instead of being invented ad hoc at the moment of blockage.
Open questions and escalation boundaries are explicit before live operator outreach begins.

tasks.title: Publish Sacramento intake rubric, trust kit, and first-capture thresholds
tasks.purpose: Give Intake, Field Ops, QA, and Rights lanes explicit Sacramento rules so they can run without founder review.
done_when: Sacramento rubric covers source quality, access-path truth, equipment/device fit, trust-packet minimums, and approval outcomes.
Sacramento first-capture thresholds and trust-kit checklist exist in one operator packet.

tasks.title: Build the Sacramento capturer prospect list and post package
tasks.purpose: Generate the first Sacramento curated professional supply wave for private controlled interiors and buyer-linked exact-site paths, then push the first real prospect or invite response into the live intake path without widening into generic gig-market posture.
done_when: A curated first-wave Sacramento professional prospect set is named with source bucket, rationale, lawful access posture, and next move.
At least one real Sacramento invite, reply, or applicant signal is landed in the live intake path with source bucket and next owner recorded.
Any copy stays draft-first and preserves no-guarantee capture language.

tasks.title: Run Sacramento public-commercial community sourcing
tasks.purpose: Open a bounded online-community sourcing lane for public, non-controlled commercial locations such as groceries, retail stores, and similar walk-in sites, and turn that lane into real intake signals.
done_when: Sacramento public-commercial sourcing names the online communities, channels, and posting brief for public, non-controlled commercial capture.
At least one live Sacramento community-sourced invite, reply, or applicant signal is landed in the intake path with source bucket and public-commercial posture recorded.
The lane stays explicitly limited to lawful public areas and preserves privacy, signage, and provenance rules.

tasks.title: Route Sacramento applicants into qualification and approval
tasks.purpose: Classify Sacramento applicants using the approved rubric instead of ad hoc founder review, and resume immediately once the first live invite or applicant signal lands.
done_when: Sacramento applicants are tagged by source bucket, approval state, and missing-trust evidence.
Exceptions are blocked with explicit missing facts instead of silently held.
If no live applicant signal exists yet, the lane is left blocked as a missing live signal rather than quietly waiting.

tasks.title: Own approved Sacramento capturers through onboarding and repeat-ready
tasks.purpose: Give every approved Sacramento mapper one routine relationship owner from approval through onboarding, first pass, and repeat-readiness so the founder is not the default support lane.
done_when: Each approved Sacramento capturer has a named lifecycle owner for approved -> onboarded -> first capture -> first pass -> repeat-ready.
Routine mapper questions, support, and coaching stay with capturer-success-agent unless they become logistics, QA, rights, privacy, or policy exceptions.

tasks.title: Assign Sacramento first captures, reminders, and site-facing trust prep
tasks.purpose: Turn approved Sacramento capturers into real first captures inside bounded thresholds.
done_when: Approved Sacramento capturers receive assignment, reminder, and site-facing trust steps through the existing field-ops lane.
Travel, timing, and access blockers are explicit on the issue and visible in the admin queue.

tasks.title: QA Sacramento first captures and route recapture decisions
tasks.purpose: Ensure Sacramento proof assets are real, clean, and ready for buyer proof work.
done_when: Sacramento captures receive PASS, BORDERLINE, or FAIL with explicit evidence.
Recapture instructions are attached when the first pass is not proof-ready.

tasks.title: Clear rights, provenance, and privacy on Sacramento proof assets
tasks.purpose: Make Sacramento proof packs releasable without weakening trust boundaries.
done_when: Each Sacramento proof asset is marked CLEARED, BLOCKED, or NEEDS-REVIEW with evidence citations.
Policy-setting exceptions route to the human reviewer and founder only when precedent changes.

tasks.title: Assemble Sacramento proof packs and publish 1-2 proof-ready listings
tasks.purpose: Turn Sacramento captures into concrete exact-site proof assets with a hosted-review path.
done_when: At least one Sacramento proof-ready listing or equivalent proof pack exists with exact-site versus adjacent-site labeling.
Each proof pack includes provenance, coverage boundaries, hosted-review path, and next-step guidance.

tasks.title: Research Sacramento robot-company target accounts and buyer clusters
tasks.purpose: Build a real Sacramento demand list that matches the proof assets Blueprint can actually show.
done_when: A named Sacramento buyer target set is researched with facility/workflow fit and proof-path notes.
Exact-site versus adjacent-site proof rules are explicit per target.

tasks.title: Prepare Sacramento proof-led outbound package and first touches
tasks.purpose: Make outbound specific to Sacramento proof assets and hosted review instead of generic AI messaging.
done_when: Sacramento outbound templates lead with one site, one workflow lane, proof-led CTA, and hosted-review next step.
First proof-led touches are queued for operator approval or event-driven send.

tasks.title: Run Sacramento outbound and move serious buyers into hosted review
tasks.purpose: Convert named Sacramento targets into serious proof conversations without dragging the founder into routine work.
done_when: Sacramento buyer conversations are active with explicit next steps.
At least one hosted proof review is run end to end or clearly blocked with named reasons.

tasks.title: Keep Sacramento buyer threads inside standard commercial handling
tasks.purpose: Prevent routine pricing and packaging questions from escalating to founder review.
done_when: Standard Sacramento quote bands, discount guardrails, and handoff thresholds are documented and used.
Only non-standard commitments escalate above the designated human commercial owner.

tasks.title: Publish the Sacramento launch scorecard and blocker view
tasks.purpose: Make Sacramento progress measurable and reviewable without relying on narrative updates.
done_when: Sacramento scorecard reports supply and demand progress against the launch thresholds.
Missing instrumentation is surfaced as blocked instead of smoothed over.

tasks.title: Mirror Sacramento execution artifacts into Notion Knowledge and Work Queue
tasks.purpose: Keep the Sacramento launch runnable and inspectable for humans outside the repo.
done_when: Sacramento execution system doc is mirrored into Notion Knowledge.
A Work Queue breadcrumb exists for the current Sacramento activation state and next human gate.

tasks.title: Run the Sacramento switch-on review before activation
tasks.purpose: Confirm the software/runtime surfaces needed by the Sacramento launch are safe before switch-on.
done_when: Sacramento switch-on review returns GO, CONDITIONAL GO, or HOLD with evidence.
Any software/runtime blocker is routed to the right engineering lane before launch activation.

tasks.title: Lock the first multi-tenant lawful access path
tasks.purpose: Secure the first complete 3-Tier Consent packet for an ambient-temperature McClellan Park industrial module.
done_when: First lawful access path is signed by owner, operator, and tenant.
